name: Recessed niche
description: A small alcove carved one or two blocks deep into an interior wall, framing a single display object such as a statue, bust, vase, or devotional figure. Inherited from Roman aedicula and refined in Renaissance palazzi and Mediterranean villas, the niche reads as a miniature architectural shrine: a flat or arched top, plain jambs, and a recessed back panel that pulls the eye into the wall thickness. In voxel terms it is a subtractive cut — air voxels removed from the perimeter wall — optionally finished with a different inner-face material to brighten the recess. Often paired with a wall lantern or torch above or beside the opening so the displayed object catches a pool of warm light, transforming the wall from a flat boundary into a chain of small, contemplative centres along the room axis.
